data.name: BID_Clubs
data.value: Choose this option if you want Clubs to be trump. You will need to win three tricks in the hand or else be "euchred" (the opposing team will get two points if that happens).

data.name: BID_Diamonds
data.value: Choose this option if you want Diamonds to be trump. You will need to win three tricks in the hand or else be "euchred" (the opposing team will get two points if that happens).

data.name: BID_GoingAlone
data.value: Choose this option if you want to play this hand without your partner's help. If you take all five tricks, you will get four points. (If the "Quiet Dealer" option is being used in this game and your partner is the dealer, then this option is automatically selected for you and cannot be changed.)

data.name: BID_Hearts
data.value: Choose this option if you want Hearts to be trump. You will need to win three tricks in the hand or else be "euchred" (the opposing team will get two points if that happens).

data.name: BID_Pass1
data.value: Choose the option if you do not want the dealer pick up the upturned card and have its suit be trump.

data.name: BID_Pass2
data.value: Choose this option if you do not want to call trump.

data.name: BID_PickItUp
data.value: Choose this option if you want the dealer to pick up the upturned card in the kitty and have its suit be trump. You will need to win three tricks in the hand or else be "euchred" (the opposing team will get two points if that happens).

data.name: BID_Spades
data.value: Choose this option if you want Spades to be trump. You will need to win three tricks in the hand or else be "euchred" (the opposing team will get two points if that happens).

data.name: Command_Exit
data.value: Are you sure you want to exit?

data.name: Command_New
data.value: Are you sure you want to start a new game?

data.name: Notice_AllPassedTwice
data.value: All players passed twice. Deal passes to the next player.

data.name: Notice_IPickItUp
data.value: {0} says "I'll pick it up." Trump is {1}.

data.name: Notice_IPickItUpAlone
data.value: {0} says "I'll pick it up -- I'm going alone!" Trump is {1}.

data.name: Notice_KittyCard
data.value: The {0} was turned up in the kitty.

data.name: Notice_PickItUpAlone
data.value: {0} says "Pick it up -- I'm going alone!" Trump is {1}.

data.name: Notice_PlayACard
data.value: Please select a card to play.

data.name: Notice_SwapACard
data.value: Please select a card to drop.

data.name: Notice_TheyEuchredYou
data.value: Their team euchred your team for two points!

data.name: Notice_TheySuperEuchredYou
data.value: Their team super-euchred your team for four points!

data.name: Notice_TheyWonTheHand
data.value: Their team won the hand for one point.

data.name: Notice_TheyWonTheHandAllTricks
data.value: Their team took all of the tricks for two points!

data.name: Notice_TheyWonTheHandAllTricksAlone
data.value: Their team took all of the tricks, going alone, for four points!

data.name: Notice_Welcome
data.value: Welcome to Matt's Euchre! Tap "New Game" to start a new game.

data.name: Notice_YouEuchredThem
data.value: Your team euchred their team for two points!

data.name: Notice_YouSuperEuchredThem
data.value: Your team super-euchred their team for four points!

data.name: Notice_YouWonTheHand
data.value: Your team won the hand for one point.

data.name: Notice_YouWonTheHandAllTricks
data.value: Your team won all the tricks for two points!

data.name: Notice_YouWonTheHandAllTricksAlone
data.value: Your team won all of the tricks, going alone, for four points!

data.name: OPTION_Conservative
data.value: If "Conservative" is selected, this player will tend to select trump only if winning the hand is guaranteed.

data.name: OPTION_Crazy
data.value: If "Crazy" is selected, this player will tend to select trump on hard-to-make hands.

data.name: OPTION_LeftOpponent
data.value: Specify a name for the player on your left here.

data.name: OPTION_LeftOpponentVoice
data.value: Specify a voice for your left opponent here.

data.name: OPTION_Name
data.value: Specify a name for yourself here.

data.name: OPTION_NineOfHearts
data.value: Checking this box will remove all of the cards of rank nine from the deck except the Nine of Hearts. (This is a variant rule used in the Appalachian area.)

data.name: OPTION_Normal
data.value: If "Normal" is selected, this player will tend to select trump only when there is a reasonable chance of winning at least three tricks.

data.name: OPTION_PeekAtOtherCards
data.value: Checking this box means that you will be able to see all card hands (plus the kitty), instead of just your own cards. This is a useful option for when you're learning how to play the game.

data.name: OPTION_QuietDealer
data.value: Checking this box means that the dealer's partner has to "go alone" if he or she selects the trump suit.

data.name: OPTION_RightOpponent
data.value: Specify a name for the player on your right here.

data.name: OPTION_ShowAnimations
data.value: Checking this box means that card-dealing and trick-taking will be animated. (Game play is slightly quicker if this is turned off.)

data.name: OPTION_RightOpponentVoice
data.value: Specify a voice for your right opponent here.

data.name: OPTION_SoundEffects
data.value: Checking this box means that you'll hear sound effects during the game. (Game play is slightly quicker if this is turned off.)

data.name: OPTION_Speech
data.value: Checking this box means that the computer players will speak during the game using text-to-speech.

data.name: OPTION_StickTheDealer
data.value: Checking this box will force the dealer to select the trump suit on the second round if no one else has called trump. The dealer's team will need to win three tricks or else be euchred, as usual.

data.name: OPTION_SuperEuchre
data.value: Checking this box means that, if the calling teams loses all five tricks, the opposing team will get 4 points instead of 2 points.

data.name: OPTION_YourPartner
data.value: Specify a name for your partner here.

data.name: OPTION_YourPartnerVoice
data.value: Specify a voice for your partner here.

data.name: SAY_MeGotFour
data.value: looks like i got some good cards that time {0}

data.name: SAY_MeGotTwo
data.value: guess i made a good call that time, huh {0}

data.name: SAY_TheyGotFour
data.value: oh, man, they got the loner!

data.name: SAY_TheyGotTwo
data.value: rats, they picked up all of the tricks.

data.name: SAY_TheyWon
data.value: oh well, nice try {0}. Shall we have another go?

data.name: SAY_WeGotEuchredMyFault
data.value: sorry, i guess i messed up on that one {0}.

data.name: SAY_WeGotEuchredOurFault
data.value: rats, guess i was just not much help {0}.

data.name: SAY_WeGotEuchredYourFault
data.value: you just might want some help from me next time on that sort of hand {0}.

data.name: SAY_WeGotFour
data.value: way to go! guess you didn't need my help {0}.

data.name: SAY_WeSuperEuchredThem
data.value: way to go {0}, we gave them the old super euchre!

data.name: SAY_WeWon
data.value: great game {0}! Shall we play again?

data.name: About_Text
data.value:
Matt's Euchre for Android
© 2006, 2013, 2026 Matthew Gertz All rights reserved.

data.name: Rules_Html
data.value:
Matt's Euchre

Written by Matthew Gertz as a programming exercise.

History of Euchre and Overview of Variations

Euchre (pronounced "YOO-ker") is a game which probably originated in Germany, with the original name "Jukerspiel" (literally, "peasant's game"). It is classified as a trick-taking game with trumps, and has similarities to Bridge, Whist, and Rook.

There are many variations of Euchre played all over the world. This software version of Euchre uses the rules common in the Midwest:

  • 24 card deck
  • No "railroading"
  • Defenders don't play alone
  • Dealer need not play alone
  • No player need have potential trump in hand in order to call trump.

Additionally, there are other supported optional rules in this software version:

  • Stick the Dealer — the dealer is forced to call trump if the bid makes it back to him/her on the second round of bidding.
  • Nine of Hearts Rule — this is a rule from northern West Virginia. In this variation, a 21-card deck is used — all 9's are thrown out except the Nine of Hearts. This means that there's only one card buried, and there are fewer trumps in three of the suits. This variation therefore favors the defenders, as they are less likely to have "junk" in their attempt to euchre the makers.
  • SuperEuchre rule — in this variation, if the defending team (the team not calling trump) takes all of the tricks, then they get four points.
  • Quiet Dealer rule — in this variation, if dealer's partner chooses trump, he/she must play alone.

Furthermore, you can set the computer-driven players' names and personalities. However, the personalities only affect bidding currently.

If you're just learning the game, I suggest that you use the "Peek" rule for the first couple of games, until you get the hang of it.

Rules of Euchre

The set-up:

A Euchre game consists of four players (in teams of two, with partners sitting opposite each other) and a deck of 24 cards. Each of the four suits in the deck contains (in descending value) an Ace, King, Queen, Jack, Ten, and Nine. (In the "Nine of Hearts" variation, only the Heart suit has a Nine.) For the trump suit, however, the ordering of the cards is Jack (same suit), Jack (other suit of same color), Ace, King, Queen, Ten, Nine. The Jacks are referred to as the right bower and left bower, respectively. (Bauer is the German word for farmer, and is also the name of their "Jack" card.) Any trump card defeats any non-trump card.

The goal:

The goal is for the team calling trump to take at least three of the five tricks in order to score points. The first team to reach 10 points wins the game.

The bid:

The dealer is chosen by passing out shuffled cards, one by one, to each player until a jack is turned up. Five cards are dealt to each player, face down, with the remaining four cards (or one card, depending on the variation used) placed near the middle in the kitty. The topmost card in the kitty is turned face up. Starting with the player to the left of the dealer, a player has the opportunity to make the suit of the face-up card to become trump (by saying "Pick it up") or to pass. If the card is ordered up, then the dealer must pick up the card and discard some other card (face down) onto the kitty. Furthermore, the suit of the picked-up card becomes trump. This is referred to as "calling trump."

If all players pass in the first round of bidding, then the kitty card is turned face-down, and each player in order is given an opportunity to pick the trump suit, which may be any suit except the one under consideration during the first round.

If all players pass on the second round, then the cards are thrown in and the deal passes to the person on the left of the current dealer. The cards are shuffled and redealt, and the process begins again. However, in the "Stick the Dealer" variation, the dealer must call trump on the second round if no one else has called trump.

Whenever trump is called, either on the first or second rounds, the person who called the trump suit has the option of "going alone," in which case his/her partner discards his hand, face down, and sits out the hand.

The team which picks the trump suit is referred to as the "making" team. The other team is the "defending" team. The "making team" must take three of the five tricks in the hand in order to "make their point."

The play:

Play begins with the player on the dealer's left. He/she leads a card, and the other players must follow suit (i.e., play a card of the same suit). (Note that the left bower counts as the same suit as the trump, not the suit pictured on it — e.g., if the trump is Spades, then the Jack of Clubs (the left bower) is also a Spade, not a Club.) If they cannot follow suit, then they can play any card. The person who plays the highest value card during the round wins the trick for his team.

The scoring:

  • If the making team took 3 or 4 tricks, then they get one point.
  • If the making team took all 5 tricks, then they get 2 points. However, if the caller decided to "go alone," then they get 4 points instead.
  • If the making team took less than 3 tricks, then the defending team is said to have "euchred" or "set" them, and the defenders thereby get 2 points.

After the hand is completed and scored, the deal passes to the player on the dealer's left, and the whole process is repeated.

The first team to reach 10 points is the winning team. (A team with 9 points is said to be "in the barn.")

Version History

Version 1.0 — 11/12/2001 (written in VB).

Version 2.0 — 10/18/2005. Rewrote code (still VB) for .NET 2.0 & VB 2005, added sound effects and speech, better window handling, menus, and better resource handling.

Version 3.0 — 12/2/2009. Rewrote code (still VB) to use WPF instead of WinForms.

Version 4.0 — 6/3/2013. Rewrote code (still VB) to use a state model.

Version 4.1 — 12/29/2014. Ported code to C#.

Version 5.0 — 2026. Ported to .NET MAUI for Android.

I hope you enjoy this game!

—Matt—
